{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27408","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27408/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27408/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27408/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27408","id":2529673095,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAA3dP857r2NO","number":27408,"title":"DOC: more informative _excluded_ argument explanation in np.vectorize","user":{"login":"vnherdeiro","id":20457174,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNDU3MTc0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20457174?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro","html_url":"https://github.com/vnherdeiro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vnherdeiro/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359990,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5OTkw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/04%20-%20Documentation","name":"04 - Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-16T23:33:44Z","updated_at":"2024-10-12T09:35:34Z","closed_at":"2024-10-12T03:31:15Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/pulls/27408","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27408","diff_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27408.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27408.patch","merged_at":"2024-10-12T03:31:15Z"},"body":"Current documentation of `exluded` argument (e.g. telling vectorize which input dimensions not to vectorize along) explains to pass the variable reference by position or name but does not advise to pass both, even though the decorated function would be more resilient to being called with a keyword and/or positional value for these non-vectorized variables.

In the documentation example:

```
    >>> def mypolyval(p, x):
    ...     _p = list(p)
    ...     res = _p.pop(0)
    ...     while _p:
    ...         res = res*x + _p.pop(0)
    ...     return res
    >>> vpolyval = np.vectorize(mypolyval, excluded=['p'])
    >>> vpolyval(p=[1, 2, 3], x=[0, 1])
    array([3, 6])
```

An error would happen if calling `vpolyval([1, 2, 3], x=[0, 1])`. The example adds `0` to the excluded member (decorator has cast `excluded` from list to set - which is not obvious until reading the code. After appending `vpolyval([1, 2, 3], x=[0, 1])` calls are working. Let's encourage practices in which the position and keywords are passed together to the decorator. I have experienced having to go through the underlying code to understand the need for it, an example passing a set of keyword and position for the same variable would have spared me some confusion and saved some time. Glad to see this merged!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2408476741/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
